2290				 * Now that we have the locks on anything added by
2291				 * get_row_security_policies, fire any RIR rules for them.
2292				 */
2293				fire_context.activeRIRs = activeRIRs;
2294				fire_context.hasRowSecurity = false;
2295
2296				expression_tree_walker((Node *) securityQuals,
2297									   fireRIRonSubLink, &fire_context);
2298
2299				expression_tree_walker((Node *) withCheckOptions,
2300									   fireRIRonSubLink, &fire_context);
2301
2302				/*
2303				 * We can ignore the value of fire_context.hasRowSecurity
2304				 * since we only reach this code in cases where hasRowSecurity
2305				 * is already true.
2306				 */
2307				Assert(hasRowSecurity);
2308
2309				activeRIRs = list_delete_last(activeRIRs);
2310			}
2311
2312			/*
2313			 * Add the new security barrier quals to the start of the RTE's
2314			 * list so that they get applied before any existing barrier quals
2315			 * (which would have come from a security-barrier view, and should
2316			 * get lower priority than RLS conditions on the table itself).
2317			 */
2318			rte->securityQuals = list_concat(securityQuals,
2319											 rte->securityQuals);
2320
2321			parsetree->withCheckOptions = list_concat(withCheckOptions,
2322													  parsetree->withCheckOptions);
2323		}
2324
2325		/*
2326		 * Make sure the query is marked correctly if row-level security
2327		 * applies, or if the new quals had sublinks.
2328		 */
2329		if (hasRowSecurity)
2330			parsetree->hasRowSecurity = true;
2331		if (hasSubLinks)
2332			parsetree->hasSubLinks = true;
2333
2334		table_close(rel, NoLock);
2335	}
2336
2337	return parsetree;
2338}
2339
2340
2341/*
2342 * Modify the given query by adding 'AND rule_qual IS NOT TRUE' to its
2343 * qualification.  This is used to generate suitable "else clauses" for
2344 * conditional INSTEAD rules.  (Unfortunately we must use "x IS NOT TRUE",
2345 * not just "NOT x" which the planner is much smarter about, else we will
2346 * do the wrong thing when the qual evaluates to NULL.)
2347 *
2348 * The rule_qual may contain references to OLD or NEW.  OLD references are
2349 * replaced by references to the specified rt_index (the relation that the
2350 * rule applies to).  NEW references are only possible for INSERT and UPDATE
2351 * queries on the relation itself, and so they should be replaced by copies
2352 * of the related entries in the query's own targetlist.
2353 */
2354static Query *
2355CopyAndAddInvertedQual(Query *parsetree,
2356					   Node *rule_qual,
2357					   int rt_index,
2358					   CmdType event)
2359{
2360	/* Don't scribble on the passed qual (it's in the relcache!) */
2361	Node	   *new_qual = copyObject(rule_qual);
2362	acquireLocksOnSubLinks_context context;
2363
2364	context.for_execute = true;
2365
2366	/*
2367	 * In case there are subqueries in the qual, acquire necessary locks and
2368	 * fix any deleted JOIN RTE entries.  (This is somewhat redundant with
2369	 * rewriteRuleAction, but not entirely ... consider restructuring so that
2370	 * we only need to process the qual this way once.)
2371	 */
2372	(void) acquireLocksOnSubLinks(new_qual, &context);
2373
2374	/* Fix references to OLD */
2375	ChangeVarNodes(new_qual, PRS2_OLD_VARNO, rt_index, 0);
2376	/* Fix references to NEW */
2377	if (event == CMD_INSERT || event == CMD_UPDATE)
2378	{
2379		RangeTblEntry *rte = rt_fetch(rt_index, parsetree->rtable);
2380		Relation	rel;
2381		List	   *gen_cols;
2382
2383		/*
2384		 * As in rewriteRuleAction, build entries for generated columns so
2385		 * that new.gen_col in the rule qualification can be rewritten
2386		 * correctly.
2387		 */
2388		rel = relation_open(rte->relid, NoLock);
2389		gen_cols = get_generated_columns(rel, PRS2_NEW_VARNO, true);
2390		relation_close(rel, NoLock);
2391
2392		/*
2393		 * The generated column expressions refer to new.attribute, so they
2394		 * must be rewritten before they can be used as replacements.
2395		 */
2396		gen_cols = (List *)
2397			ReplaceVarsFromTargetList((Node *) gen_cols,
2398									  PRS2_NEW_VARNO,
2399									  0,
2400									  rte,
2401									  parsetree->targetList,
2402									  parsetree->resultRelation,
2403									  (event == CMD_UPDATE) ?
2404									  REPLACEVARS_CHANGE_VARNO :
2405									  REPLACEVARS_SUBSTITUTE_NULL,
2406									  rt_index,
2407									  &parsetree->hasSubLinks);
2408
2409		new_qual = ReplaceVarsFromTargetList(new_qual,
2410											 PRS2_NEW_VARNO,
2411											 0,
2412											 rte,
2413											 list_concat(gen_cols,
2414														 parsetree->targetList),
2415											 parsetree->resultRelation,
2416											 (event == CMD_UPDATE) ?
2417											 REPLACEVARS_CHANGE_VARNO :
2418											 REPLACEVARS_SUBSTITUTE_NULL,
2419											 rt_index,
2420											 &parsetree->hasSubLinks);
2421	}
2422	/* And attach the fixed qual */
2423	AddInvertedQual(parsetree, new_qual);
2424
2425	return parsetree;
2426}
2427
2428
2429/*
2430 *	fireRules -
2431 *	   Iterate through rule locks applying rules.
2432 *
2433 * Input arguments:
2434 *	parsetree - original query
2435 *	rt_index - RT index of result relation in original query
2436 *	event - type of rule event
2437 *	locks - list of rules to fire
2438 * Output arguments:
2439 *	*instead_flag - set true if any unqualified INSTEAD rule is found
2440 *					(must be initialized to false)
2441 *	*returning_flag - set true if we rewrite RETURNING clause in any rule
2442 *					(must be initialized to false)
2443 *	*qual_product - filled with modified original query if any qualified
2444 *					INSTEAD rule is found (must be initialized to NULL)
2445 * Return value:
2446 *	list of rule actions adjusted for use with this query
2447 *
2448 * Qualified INSTEAD rules generate their action with the qualification
2449 * condition added.  They also generate a modified version of the original
2450 * query with the negated qualification added, so that it will run only for
2451 * rows that the qualified action doesn't act on.  (If there are multiple
2452 * qualified INSTEAD rules, we AND all the negated quals onto a single
2453 * modified original query.)  We won't execute the original, unmodified
2454 * query if we find either qualified or unqualified INSTEAD rules.  If
2455 * we find both, the modified original query is discarded too.
2456 */
2457static List *
2458fireRules(Query *parsetree,
2459		  int rt_index,
2460		  CmdType event,
2461		  List *locks,
2462		  bool *instead_flag,
2463		  bool *returning_flag,
2464		  Query **qual_product)
2465{
2466	List	   *results = NIL;
2467	ListCell   *l;
2468
2469	foreach(l, locks)
2470	{
2471		RewriteRule *rule_lock = (RewriteRule *) lfirst(l);
2472		Node	   *event_qual = rule_lock->qual;
2473		List	   *actions = rule_lock->actions;
2474		QuerySource qsrc;
2475		ListCell   *r;
2476
2477		/* Determine correct QuerySource value for actions */
2478		if (rule_lock->isInstead)
2479		{
2480			if (event_qual != NULL)
2481				qsrc = QSRC_QUAL_INSTEAD_RULE;
2482			else
2483			{
2484				qsrc = QSRC_INSTEAD_RULE;
2485				*instead_flag = true;	/* report unqualified INSTEAD */
2486			}
2487		}
2488		else
2489			qsrc = QSRC_NON_INSTEAD_RULE;
2490
2491		if (qsrc == QSRC_QUAL_INSTEAD_RULE)
2492		{
2493			/*
2494			 * If there are INSTEAD rules with qualifications, the original
2495			 * query is still performed. But all the negated rule
2496			 * qualifications of the INSTEAD rules are added so it does its
2497			 * actions only in cases where the rule quals of all INSTEAD rules
2498			 * are false. Think of it as the default action in a case. We save
2499			 * this in *qual_product so RewriteQuery() can add it to the query
2500			 * list after we mangled it up enough.
2501			 *
2502			 * If we have already found an unqualified INSTEAD rule, then
2503			 * *qual_product won't be used, so don't bother building it.
2504			 */
2505			if (!*instead_flag)
2506			{
2507				if (*qual_product == NULL)
2508					*qual_product = copyObject(parsetree);
2509				*qual_product = CopyAndAddInvertedQual(*qual_product,
2510													   event_qual,
2511													   rt_index,
2512													   event);
2513			}
2514		}
2515
2516		/* Now process the rule's actions and add them to the result list */
2517		foreach(r, actions)
2518		{
2519			Query	   *rule_action = lfirst(r);
2520
2521			if (rule_action->commandType == CMD_NOTHING)
2522				continue;
2523
2524			rule_action = rewriteRuleAction(parsetree, rule_action,
2525											event_qual, rt_index, event,
2526											returning_flag);
2527
2528			rule_action->querySource = qsrc;
2529			rule_action->canSetTag = false; /* might change later */
2530
2531			results = lappend(results, rule_action);
2532		}
2533	}
2534
2535	return results;
2536}
2537
2538
2539/*
2540 * get_view_query - get the Query from a view's _RETURN rule.
2541 *
2542 * Caller should have verified that the relation is a view, and therefore
2543 * we should find an ON SELECT action.
2544 *
2545 * Note that the pointer returned is into the relcache and therefore must
2546 * be treated as read-only to the caller and not modified or scribbled on.
2547 */
2548Query *
2549get_view_query(Relation view)
2550{
2551	int			i;
2552
2553	Assert(view->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW);
2554
2555	for (i = 0; i < view->rd_rules->numLocks; i++)
2556	{
2557		RewriteRule *rule = view->rd_rules->rules[i];
2558
2559		if (rule->event == CMD_SELECT)
2560		{
2561			/* A _RETURN rule should have only one action */
2562			if (list_length(rule->actions) != 1)
2563				elog(ERROR, "invalid _RETURN rule action specification");
2564
2565			return (Query *) linitial(rule->actions);
2566		}
2567	}
2568
2569	elog(ERROR, "failed to find _RETURN rule for view");
2570	return NULL;				/* keep compiler quiet */
2571}
2572
2573
2574/*
2575 * view_has_instead_trigger - does view have an INSTEAD OF trigger for event?
2576 *
2577 * If it does, we don't want to treat it as auto-updatable.  This test can't
2578 * be folded into view_query_is_auto_updatable because it's not an error
2579 * condition.
2580 *
2581 * For MERGE, this will return true if there is an INSTEAD OF trigger for
2582 * every action in mergeActionList, and false if there are any actions that
2583 * lack an INSTEAD OF trigger.  If there are no data-modifying MERGE actions
2584 * (only DO NOTHING actions), true is returned so that the view is treated
2585 * as trigger-updatable, rather than erroring out if it's not auto-updatable.
2586 */
2587bool
2588view_has_instead_trigger(Relation view, CmdType event, List *mergeActionList)
2589{
2590	TriggerDesc *trigDesc = view->trigdesc;
2591
2592	switch (event)
2593	{
2594		case CMD_INSERT:
2595			if (trigDesc && trigDesc->trig_insert_instead_row)
2596				return true;
2597			break;
2598		case CMD_UPDATE:
2599			if (trigDesc && trigDesc->trig_update_instead_row)
2600				return true;
2601			break;
2602		case CMD_DELETE:
2603			if (trigDesc && trigDesc->trig_delete_instead_row)
2604				return true;
2605			break;
2606		case CMD_MERGE:
2607			foreach_node(MergeAction, action, mergeActionList)
2608			{
2609				switch (action->commandType)
2610				{
2611					case CMD_INSERT:
2612						if (!trigDesc || !trigDesc->trig_insert_instead_row)
2613							return false;
2614						break;
2615					case CMD_UPDATE:
2616						if (!trigDesc || !trigDesc->trig_update_instead_row)
2617							return false;
2618						break;
2619					case CMD_DELETE:
2620						if (!trigDesc || !trigDesc->trig_delete_instead_row)
2621							return false;
2622						break;
2623					case CMD_NOTHING:
2624						/* No trigger required */
2625						break;
2626					default:
2627						elog(ERROR, "unrecognized commandType: %d", action->commandType);
2628						break;
2629				}
2630			}
2631			return true;		/* no actions without an INSTEAD OF trigger */
2632		default:
2633			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized CmdType: %d", (int) event);
2634			break;
2635	}
2636	return false;
2637}
2638
2639
2640/*
2641 * view_col_is_auto_updatable - test whether the specified column of a view
2642 * is auto-updatable. Returns NULL (if the column can be updated) or a message
2643 * string giving the reason that it cannot be.
2644 *
2645 * The returned string has not been translated; if it is shown as an error
2646 * message, the caller should apply _() to translate it.
2647 *
2648 * Note that the checks performed here are local to this view. We do not check
2649 * whether the referenced column of the underlying base relation is updatable.
2650 */
2651static const char *
2652view_col_is_auto_updatable(RangeTblRef *rtr, TargetEntry *tle)
2653{
2654	Var		   *var = (Var *) tle->expr;
2655
2656	/*
2657	 * For now, the only updatable columns we support are those that are Vars
2658	 * referring to user columns of the underlying base relation.
2659	 *
2660	 * The view targetlist may contain resjunk columns (e.g., a view defined
2661	 * like "SELECT * FROM t ORDER BY a+b" is auto-updatable) but such columns
2662	 * are not auto-updatable, and in fact should never appear in the outer
2663	 * query's targetlist.
2664	 */
2665	if (tle->resjunk)
2666		return gettext_noop("Junk view columns are not updatable.");
2667
2668	if (!IsA(var, Var) ||
2669		var->varno != rtr->rtindex ||
2670		var->varlevelsup != 0)
2671		return gettext_noop("View columns that are not columns of their base relation are not updatable.");
2672
2673	if (var->varattno < 0)
2674		return gettext_noop("View columns that refer to system columns are not updatable.");
2675
2676	if (var->varattno == 0)
2677		return gettext_noop("View columns that return whole-row references are not updatable.");
2678
2679	return NULL;				/* the view column is updatable */
2680}
2681
2682
2683/*
2684 * view_query_is_auto_updatable - test whether the specified view definition
2685 * represents an auto-updatable view. Returns NULL (if the view can be updated)
2686 * or a message string giving the reason that it cannot be.
2687
2688 * The returned string has not been translated; if it is shown as an error
2689 * message, the caller should apply _() to translate it.
2690 *
2691 * If check_cols is true, the view is required to have at least one updatable
2692 * column (necessary for INSERT/UPDATE). Otherwise the view's columns are not
2693 * checked for updatability. See also view_cols_are_auto_updatable.
2694 *
2695 * Note that the checks performed here are only based on the view definition.
2696 * We do not check whether any base relations referred to by the view are
2697 * updatable.
2698 */
2699const char *
2700view_query_is_auto_updatable(Query *viewquery, bool check_cols)
2701{
2702	RangeTblRef *rtr;
2703	RangeTblEntry *base_rte;
2704
2705	/*----------
2706	 * Check if the view is simply updatable.  According to SQL-92 this means:
2707	 *	- No DISTINCT clause.
2708	 *	- Each TLE is a column reference, and each column appears at most once.
2709	 *	- FROM contains exactly one base relation.
2710	 *	- No GROUP BY or HAVING clauses.
2711	 *	- No set operations (UNION, INTERSECT or EXCEPT).
2712	 *	- No sub-queries in the WHERE clause that reference the target table.
2713	 *
2714	 * We ignore that last restriction since it would be complex to enforce
2715	 * and there isn't any actual benefit to disallowing sub-queries.  (The
2716	 * semantic issues that the standard is presumably concerned about don't
2717	 * arise in Postgres, since any such sub-query will not see any updates
2718	 * executed by the outer query anyway, thanks to MVCC snapshotting.)
2719	 *
2720	 * We also relax the second restriction by supporting part of SQL:1999
2721	 * feature T111, which allows for a mix of updatable and non-updatable
2722	 * columns, provided that an INSERT or UPDATE doesn't attempt to assign to
2723	 * a non-updatable column.
2724	 *
2725	 * In addition we impose these constraints, involving features that are
2726	 * not part of SQL-92:
2727	 *	- No CTEs (WITH clauses).
2728	 *	- No OFFSET or LIMIT clauses (this matches a SQL:2008 restriction).
2729	 *	- No system columns (including whole-row references) in the tlist.
2730	 *	- No window functions in the tlist.
2731	 *	- No set-returning functions in the tlist.
2732	 *
2733	 * Note that we do these checks without recursively expanding the view.
2734	 * If the base relation is a view, we'll recursively deal with it later.
2735	 *----------
2736	 */
2737	if (viewquery->distinctClause != NIL)
2738		return gettext_noop("Views containing DISTINCT are not automatically updatable.");
2739
2740	if (viewquery->groupClause != NIL || viewquery->groupingSets)
2741		return gettext_noop("Views containing GROUP BY are not automatically updatable.");
2742
2743	if (viewquery->havingQual != NULL)
2744		return gettext_noop("Views containing HAVING are not automatically updatable.");
2745
2746	if (viewquery->setOperations != NULL)
2747		return gettext_noop("Views containing UNION, INTERSECT, or EXCEPT are not automatically updatable.");
2748
2749	if (viewquery->cteList != NIL)
2750		return gettext_noop("Views containing WITH are not automatically updatable.");
2751
2752	if (viewquery->limitOffset != NULL || viewquery->limitCount != NULL)
2753		return gettext_noop("Views containing LIMIT or OFFSET are not automatically updatable.");
2754
2755	/*
2756	 * We must not allow window functions or set returning functions in the
2757	 * targetlist. Otherwise we might end up inserting them into the quals of
2758	 * the main query. We must also check for aggregates in the targetlist in
2759	 * case they appear without a GROUP BY.
2760	 *
2761	 * These restrictions ensure that each row of the view corresponds to a
2762	 * unique row in the underlying base relation.
2763	 */
2764	if (viewquery->hasAggs)
2765		return gettext_noop("Views that return aggregate functions are not automatically updatable.");
2766
2767	if (viewquery->hasWindowFuncs)
2768		return gettext_noop("Views that return window functions are not automatically updatable.");
2769
2770	if (viewquery->hasTargetSRFs)
2771		return gettext_noop("Views that return set-returning functions are not automatically updatable.");
2772
2773	/*
2774	 * The view query should select from a single base relation, which must be
2775	 * a table or another view.
2776	 */
2777	if (list_length(viewquery->jointree->fromlist) != 1)
2778		return gettext_noop("Views that do not select from a single table or view are not automatically updatable.");
2779
2780	rtr = (RangeTblRef *) linitial(viewquery->jointree->fromlist);
2781	if (!IsA(rtr, RangeTblRef))
2782		return gettext_noop("Views that do not select from a single table or view are not automatically updatable.");
2783
2784	base_rte = rt_fetch(rtr->rtindex, viewquery->rtable);
2785	if (base_rte->rtekind != RTE_RELATION ||
2786		(base_rte->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
2787		 base_rte->relkind != RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE &&
2788		 base_rte->relkind != RELKIND_VIEW &&
2789		 base_rte->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE))
2790		return gettext_noop("Views that do not select from a single table or view are not automatically updatable.");
2791
2792	if (base_rte->tablesample)
2793		return gettext_noop("Views containing TABLESAMPLE are not automatically updatable.");
2794
2795	/*
2796	 * Check that the view has at least one updatable column. This is required
2797	 * for INSERT/UPDATE but not for DELETE.
2798	 */
2799	if (check_cols)
2800	{
2801		ListCell   *cell;
2802		bool		found;
2803
2804		found = false;
2805		foreach(cell, viewquery->targetList)
2806		{
2807			TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(cell);
2808
2809			if (view_col_is_auto_updatable(rtr, tle) == NULL)
2810			{
2811				found = true;
2812				break;
2813			}
2814		}
2815
2816		if (!found)
2817			return gettext_noop("Views that have no updatable columns are not automatically updatable.");
2818	}
2819
2820	return NULL;				/* the view is updatable */
2821}
2822
2823
2824/*
2825 * view_cols_are_auto_updatable - test whether all of the required columns of
2826 * an auto-updatable view are actually updatable. Returns NULL (if all the
2827 * required columns can be updated) or a message string giving the reason that
2828 * they cannot be.
2829 *
2830 * The returned string has not been translated; if it is shown as an error
2831 * message, the caller should apply _() to translate it.
2832 *
2833 * This should be used for INSERT/UPDATE to ensure that we don't attempt to
2834 * assign to any non-updatable columns.
2835 *
2836 * Additionally it may be used to retrieve the set of updatable columns in the
2837 * view, or if one or more of the required columns is not updatable, the name
2838 * of the first offending non-updatable column.
2839 *
2840 * The caller must have already verified that this is an auto-updatable view
2841 * using view_query_is_auto_updatable.
2842 *
2843 * Note that the checks performed here are only based on the view definition.
2844 * We do not check whether the referenced columns of the base relation are
2845 * updatable.
2846 */
2847static const char *
2848view_cols_are_auto_updatable(Query *viewquery,
2849							 Bitmapset *required_cols,
2850							 Bitmapset **updatable_cols,
2851							 char **non_updatable_col)
2852{
2853	RangeTblRef *rtr;
2854	AttrNumber	col;
2855	ListCell   *cell;
2856
2857	/*
2858	 * The caller should have verified that this view is auto-updatable and so
2859	 * there should be a single base relation.
2860	 */
2861	Assert(list_length(viewquery->jointree->fromlist) == 1);
2862	rtr = linitial_node(RangeTblRef, viewquery->jointree->fromlist);
2863
2864	/* Initialize the optional return values */
2865	if (updatable_cols != NULL)
2866		*updatable_cols = NULL;
2867	if (non_updatable_col != NULL)
2868		*non_updatable_col = NULL;
2869
2870	/* Test each view column for updatability */
2871	col = -FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;
2872	foreach(cell, viewquery->targetList)
2873	{
2874		TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(cell);
2875		const char *col_update_detail;
2876
2877		col++;
2878		col_update_detail = view_col_is_auto_updatable(rtr, tle);
2879
2880		if (col_update_detail == NULL)
2881		{
2882			/* The column is updatable */
2883			if (updatable_cols != NULL)
2884				*updatable_cols = bms_add_member(*updatable_cols, col);
2885		}
2886		else if (bms_is_member(col, required_cols))
2887		{
2888			/* The required column is not updatable */
2889			if (non_updatable_col != NULL)
2890				*non_updatable_col = tle->resname;
2891			return col_update_detail;
2892		}
2893	}
2894
2895	return NULL;				/* all the required view columns are updatable */
2896}
2897
2898
2899/*
2900 * relation_is_updatable - determine which update events the specified
2901 * relation supports.
2902 *
2903 * Note that views may contain a mix of updatable and non-updatable columns.
2904 * For a view to support INSERT/UPDATE it must have at least one updatable
2905 * column, but there is no such restriction for DELETE. If include_cols is
2906 * non-NULL, then only the specified columns are considered when testing for
2907 * updatability.
2908 *
2909 * Unlike the preceding functions, this does recurse to look at a view's
2910 * base relations, so it needs to detect recursion.  To do that, we pass
2911 * a list of currently-considered outer relations.  External callers need
2912 * only pass NIL.
2913 *
2914 * This is used for the information_schema views, which have separate concepts
2915 * of "updatable" and "trigger updatable".  A relation is "updatable" if it
2916 * can be updated without the need for triggers (either because it has a
2917 * suitable RULE, or because it is simple enough to be automatically updated).
2918 * A relation is "trigger updatable" if it has a suitable INSTEAD OF trigger.
2919 * The SQL standard regards this as not necessarily updatable, presumably
2920 * because there is no way of knowing what the trigger will actually do.
2921 * The information_schema views therefore call this function with
2922 * include_triggers = false.  However, other callers might only care whether
2923 * data-modifying SQL will work, so they can pass include_triggers = true
2924 * to have trigger updatability included in the result.
2925 *
2926 * The return value is a bitmask of rule event numbers indicating which of
2927 * the INSERT, UPDATE and DELETE operations are supported.  (We do it this way
2928 * so that we can test for UPDATE plus DELETE support in a single call.)
2929 */
2930int
2931relation_is_updatable(Oid reloid,
2932					  List *outer_reloids,
2933					  bool include_triggers,
2934					  Bitmapset *include_cols)
2935{
2936	int			events = 0;
2937	Relation	rel;
2938	RuleLock   *rulelocks;
2939
2940#define ALL_EVENTS ((1 << CMD_INSERT) | (1 << CMD_UPDATE) | (1 << CMD_DELETE))
2941
2942	/* Since this function recurses, it could be driven to stack overflow */
2943	check_stack_depth();
2944
2945	rel = try_relation_open(reloid, AccessShareLock);
2946
2947	/*
2948	 * If the relation doesn't exist, return zero rather than throwing an
2949	 * error.  This is helpful since scanning an information_schema view under
2950	 * MVCC rules can result in referencing rels that have actually been
2951	 * deleted already.
2952	 */
2953	if (rel == NULL)
2954		return 0;
2955
2956	/* If we detect a recursive view, report that it is not updatable */
2957	if (list_member_oid(outer_reloids, RelationGetRelid(rel)))
2958	{
2959		relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
2960		return 0;
2961	}
2962
2963	/* If the relation is a table, it is always updatable */
2964	if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_RELATION ||
2965		rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
2966	{
2967		relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
2968		return ALL_EVENTS;
2969	}
2970
2971	/* Look for unconditional DO INSTEAD rules, and note supported events */
2972	rulelocks = rel->rd_rules;
2973	if (rulelocks != NULL)
2974	{
2975		int			i;
2976
2977		for (i = 0; i < rulelocks->numLocks; i++)
2978		{
2979			if (rulelocks->rules[i]->isInstead &&
2980				rulelocks->rules[i]->qual == NULL)
2981			{
2982				events |= ((1 << rulelocks->rules[i]->event) & ALL_EVENTS);
2983			}
2984		}
2985
2986		/* If we have rules for all events, we're done */
2987		if (events == ALL_EVENTS)
2988		{
2989			relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
2990			return events;
2991		}
2992	}
2993
2994	/* Similarly look for INSTEAD OF triggers, if they are to be included */
2995	if (include_triggers)
2996	{
2997		TriggerDesc *trigDesc = rel->trigdesc;
2998
2999		if (trigDesc)
3000		{
3001			if (trigDesc->trig_insert_instead_row)
3002				events |= (1 << CMD_INSERT);
3003			if (trigDesc->trig_update_instead_row)
3004				events |= (1 << CMD_UPDATE);
3005			if (trigDesc->trig_delete_instead_row)
3006				events |= (1 << CMD_DELETE);
3007
3008			/* If we have triggers for all events, we're done */
3009			if (events == ALL_EVENTS)
3010			{
3011				relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
3012				return events;
3013			}
3014		}
3015	}
3016
3017	/* If this is a foreign table, check which update events it supports */
3018	if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)
3019	{
3020		FdwRoutine *fdwroutine = GetFdwRoutineForRelation(rel, false);
3021
3022		if (fdwroutine->IsForeignRelUpdatable != NULL)
3023			events |= fdwroutine->IsForeignRelUpdatable(rel);
3024		else
3025		{
3026			/* Assume presence of executor functions is sufficient */
3027			if (fdwroutine->ExecForeignInsert != NULL)
3028				events |= (1 << CMD_INSERT);
3029			if (fdwroutine->ExecForeignUpdate != NULL)
3030				events |= (1 << CMD_UPDATE);
3031			if (fdwroutine->ExecForeignDelete != NULL)
3032				events |= (1 << CMD_DELETE);
3033		}
3034
3035		relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
3036		return events;
3037	}
3038
3039	/* Check if this is an automatically updatable view */
3040	if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW)
3041	{
3042		Query	   *viewquery = get_view_query(rel);
3043
3044		if (view_query_is_auto_updatable(viewquery, false) == NULL)
3045		{
3046			Bitmapset  *updatable_cols;
3047			int			auto_events;
3048			RangeTblRef *rtr;
3049			RangeTblEntry *base_rte;
3050			Oid			baseoid;
3051
3052			/*
3053			 * Determine which of the view's columns are updatable. If there
3054			 * are none within the set of columns we are looking at, then the
3055			 * view doesn't support INSERT/UPDATE, but it may still support
3056			 * DELETE.
3057			 */
3058			view_cols_are_auto_updatable(viewquery, NULL,
3059										 &updatable_cols, NULL);
3060
3061			if (include_cols != NULL)
3062				updatable_cols = bms_int_members(updatable_cols, include_cols);
3063
3064			if (bms_is_empty(updatable_cols))
3065				auto_events = (1 << CMD_DELETE);	/* May support DELETE */
3066			else
3067				auto_events = ALL_EVENTS;	/* May support all events */
3068
3069			/*
3070			 * The base relation must also support these update commands.
3071			 * Tables are always updatable, but for any other kind of base
3072			 * relation we must do a recursive check limited to the columns
3073			 * referenced by the locally updatable columns in this view.
3074			 */
3075			rtr = (RangeTblRef *) linitial(viewquery->jointree->fromlist);
3076			base_rte = rt_fetch(rtr->rtindex, viewquery->rtable);
3077			Assert(base_rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
3078
3079			if (base_rte->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
3080				base_rte->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
3081			{
3082				baseoid = base_rte->relid;
3083				outer_reloids = lappend_oid(outer_reloids,
3084											RelationGetRelid(rel));
3085				include_cols = adjust_view_column_set(updatable_cols,
3086													  viewquery->targetList);
3087				auto_events &= relation_is_updatable(baseoid,
3088													 outer_reloids,
3089													 include_triggers,
3090													 include_cols);
3091				outer_reloids = list_delete_last(outer_reloids);
3092			}
3093			events |= auto_events;
3094		}
3095	}
3096
3097	/* If we reach here, the relation may support some update commands */
3098	relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
3099	return events;
3100}
3101
3102
3103/*
3104 * adjust_view_column_set - map a set of column numbers according to targetlist
3105 *
3106 * This is used with simply-updatable views to map column-permissions sets for
3107 * the view columns onto the matching columns in the underlying base relation.
3108 * Relevant entries in the targetlist must be plain Vars of the underlying
3109 * relation (as per the checks above in view_query_is_auto_updatable).
3110 */
3111static Bitmapset *
3112adjust_view_column_set(Bitmapset *cols, List *targetlist)
3113{
3114	Bitmapset  *result = NULL;
3115	int			col;
3116
3117	col = -1;
3118	while ((col = bms_next_member(cols, col)) >= 0)
3119	{
3120		/* bit numbers are offset by FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber */
3121		AttrNumber	attno = col + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;
3122
3123		if (attno == InvalidAttrNumber)
3124		{
3125			/*
3126			 * There's a whole-row reference to the view.  For permissions
3127			 * purposes, treat it as a reference to each column available from
3128			 * the view.  (We should *not* convert this to a whole-row
3129			 * reference to the base relation, since the view may not touch
3130			 * all columns of the base relation.)
3131			 */
3132			ListCell   *lc;
3133
3134			foreach(lc, targetlist)
3135			{
3136				TargetEntry *tle = lfirst_node(TargetEntry, lc);
3137				Var		   *var;
3138
3139				if (tle->resjunk)
3140					continue;
3141				var = castNode(Var, tle->expr);
3142				result = bms_add_member(result,
3143										var->varattno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
3144			}
3145		}
3146		else
3147		{
3148			/*
3149			 * Views do not have system columns, so we do not expect to see
3150			 * any other system attnos here.  If we do find one, the error
3151			 * case will apply.
3152			 */
3153			TargetEntry *tle = get_tle_by_resno(targetlist, attno);
3154
3155			if (tle != NULL && !tle->resjunk && IsA(tle->expr, Var))
3156			{
3157				Var		   *var = (Var *) tle->expr;
3158
3159				result = bms_add_member(result,
3160										var->varattno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
3161			}
3162			else
3163				elog(ERROR, "attribute number %d not found in view targetlist",
3164					 attno);
3165		}
3166	}
3167
3168	return result;
3169}
3170
3171
3172/*
3173 * error_view_not_updatable -
3174 *	  Report an error due to an attempt to update a non-updatable view.
3175 *
3176 * Generally this is expected to be called from the rewriter, with suitable
3177 * error detail explaining why the view is not updatable.  Note, however, that
3178 * the executor also performs a just-in-case check that the target view is
3179 * updatable.  That check is expected to never fail, but if it does, it will
3180 * call this function with NULL error detail --- see CheckValidResultRel().
3181 *
3182 * Note: for MERGE, at least one of the actions in mergeActionList is expected
3183 * to lack a suitable INSTEAD OF trigger --- see view_has_instead_trigger().
3184 */
3185void
3186error_view_not_updatable(Relation view,
3187						 CmdType command,
3188						 List *mergeActionList,
3189						 const char *detail)
3190{
3191	TriggerDesc *trigDesc = view->trigdesc;
3192
3193	switch (command)
3194	{
3195		case CMD_INSERT:
3196			ereport(ERROR,
3197					errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3198					errmsg("cannot insert into view \"%s\"",
3199						   RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3200					detail ? errdetail_internal("%s", _(detail)) : 0,
3201					errhint("To enable inserting into the view, provide an INSTEAD OF INSERT trigger or an unconditional ON INSERT DO INSTEAD rule."));
3202			break;
3203		case CMD_UPDATE:
3204			ereport(ERROR,
3205					errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3206					errmsg("cannot update view \"%s\"",
3207						   RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3208					detail ? errdetail_internal("%s", _(detail)) : 0,
3209					errhint("To enable updating the view, provide an INSTEAD OF UPDATE trigger or an unconditional ON UPDATE DO INSTEAD rule."));
3210			break;
3211		case CMD_DELETE:
3212			ereport(ERROR,
3213					errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3214					errmsg("cannot delete from view \"%s\"",
3215						   RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3216					detail ? errdetail_internal("%s", _(detail)) : 0,
3217					errhint("To enable deleting from the view, provide an INSTEAD OF DELETE trigger or an unconditional ON DELETE DO INSTEAD rule."));
3218			break;
3219		case CMD_MERGE:
3220
3221			/*
3222			 * Note that the error hints here differ from above, since MERGE
3223			 * doesn't support rules.
3224			 */
3225			foreach_node(MergeAction, action, mergeActionList)
3226			{
3227				switch (action->commandType)
3228				{
3229					case CMD_INSERT:
3230						if (!trigDesc || !trigDesc->trig_insert_instead_row)
3231							ereport(ERROR,
3232									errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3233									errmsg("cannot insert into view \"%s\"",
3234										   RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3235									detail ? errdetail_internal("%s", _(detail)) : 0,
3236									errhint("To enable inserting into the view using MERGE, provide an INSTEAD OF INSERT trigger."));
3237						break;
3238					case CMD_UPDATE:
3239						if (!trigDesc || !trigDesc->trig_update_instead_row)
3240							ereport(ERROR,
3241									errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3242									errmsg("cannot update view \"%s\"",
3243										   RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3244									detail ? errdetail_internal("%s", _(detail)) : 0,
3245									errhint("To enable updating the view using MERGE, provide an INSTEAD OF UPDATE trigger."));
3246						break;
3247					case CMD_DELETE:
3248						if (!trigDesc || !trigDesc->trig_delete_instead_row)
3249							ereport(ERROR,
3250									errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3251									errmsg("cannot delete from view \"%s\"",
3252										   RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3253									detail ? errdetail_internal("%s", _(detail)) : 0,
3254									errhint("To enable deleting from the view using MERGE, provide an INSTEAD OF DELETE trigger."));
3255						break;
3256					case CMD_NOTHING:
3257						break;
3258					default:
3259						elog(ERROR, "unrecognized commandType: %d", action->commandType);
3260						break;
3261				}
3262			}
3263			break;
3264		default:
3265			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized CmdType: %d", (int) command);
3266			break;
3267	}
3268}
3269
3270
3271/*
3272 * rewriteTargetView -
3273 *	  Attempt to rewrite a query where the target relation is a view, so that
3274 *	  the view's base relation becomes the target relation.
3275 *
3276 * Note that the base relation here may itself be a view, which may or may not
3277 * have INSTEAD OF triggers or rules to handle the update.  That is handled by
3278 * the recursion in RewriteQuery.
3279 */
3280static Query *
3281rewriteTargetView(Query *parsetree, Relation view)
3282{
3283	Query	   *viewquery;
3284	bool		insert_or_update;
3285	const char *auto_update_detail;
3286	RangeTblRef *rtr;
3287	int			base_rt_index;
3288	int			new_rt_index;
3289	RangeTblEntry *base_rte;
3290	RangeTblEntry *view_rte;
3291	RangeTblEntry *new_rte;
3292	RTEPermissionInfo *base_perminfo;
3293	RTEPermissionInfo *view_perminfo;
3294	RTEPermissionInfo *new_perminfo;
3295	Relation	base_rel;
3296	List	   *view_targetlist;
3297	ListCell   *lc;
3298
3299	/*
3300	 * Get the Query from the view's ON SELECT rule.  We're going to munge the
3301	 * Query to change the view's base relation into the target relation,
3302	 * along with various other changes along the way, so we need to make a
3303	 * copy of it (get_view_query() returns a pointer into the relcache, so we
3304	 * have to treat it as read-only).
3305	 */
3306	viewquery = copyObject(get_view_query(view));
3307
3308	/* Locate RTE and perminfo describing the view in the outer query */
3309	view_rte = rt_fetch(parsetree->resultRelation, parsetree->rtable);
3310	view_perminfo = getRTEPermissionInfo(parsetree->rteperminfos, view_rte);
3311
3312	/*
3313	 * Are we doing INSERT/UPDATE, or MERGE containing INSERT/UPDATE?  If so,
3314	 * various additional checks on the view columns need to be applied, and
3315	 * any view CHECK OPTIONs need to be enforced.
3316	 */
3317	insert_or_update =
3318		(parsetree->commandType == CMD_INSERT ||
3319		 parsetree->commandType == CMD_UPDATE);
3320
3321	if (parsetree->commandType == CMD_MERGE)
3322	{
3323		foreach_node(MergeAction, action, parsetree->mergeActionList)
3324		{
3325			if (action->commandType == CMD_INSERT ||
3326				action->commandType == CMD_UPDATE)
3327			{
3328				insert_or_update = true;
3329				break;
3330			}
3331		}
3332	}
3333
3334	/* Check if the expansion of non-system views are restricted */
3335	if (unlikely((restrict_nonsystem_relation_kind & RESTRICT_RELKIND_VIEW) != 0 &&
3336				 RelationGetRelid(view) >= FirstNormalObjectId))
3337		ereport(ERROR,
3338				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3339				 errmsg("access to non-system view \"%s\" is restricted",
3340						RelationGetRelationName(view))));
3341
3342	/*
3343	 * The view must be updatable, else fail.
3344	 *
3345	 * If we are doing INSERT/UPDATE (or MERGE containing INSERT/UPDATE), we
3346	 * also check that there is at least one updatable column.
3347	 */
3348	auto_update_detail =
3349		view_query_is_auto_updatable(viewquery, insert_or_update);
3350
3351	if (auto_update_detail)
3352		error_view_not_updatable(view,
3353								 parsetree->commandType,
3354								 parsetree->mergeActionList,
3355								 auto_update_detail);
3356
3357	/*
3358	 * For INSERT/UPDATE (or MERGE containing INSERT/UPDATE) the modified
3359	 * columns must all be updatable.
3360	 */
3361	if (insert_or_update)
3362	{
3363		Bitmapset  *modified_cols;
3364		char	   *non_updatable_col;
3365
3366		/*
3367		 * Compute the set of modified columns as those listed in the result
3368		 * RTE's insertedCols and/or updatedCols sets plus those that are
3369		 * targets of the query's targetlist(s).  We must consider the query's
3370		 * targetlist because rewriteTargetListIU may have added additional
3371		 * targetlist entries for view defaults, and these must also be
3372		 * updatable.  But rewriteTargetListIU can also remove entries if they
3373		 * are DEFAULT markers and the column's default is NULL, so
3374		 * considering only the targetlist would also be wrong.
3375		 */
3376		modified_cols = bms_union(view_perminfo->insertedCols,
3377								  view_perminfo->updatedCols);
3378
3379		foreach(lc, parsetree->targetList)
3380		{
3381			TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
3382
3383			if (!tle->resjunk)
3384				modified_cols = bms_add_member(modified_cols,
3385											   tle->resno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
3386		}
3387
3388		if (parsetree->onConflict)
3389		{
3390			foreach(lc, parsetree->onConflict->onConflictSet)
3391			{
3392				TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
3393
3394				if (!tle->resjunk)
3395					modified_cols = bms_add_member(modified_cols,
3396												   tle->resno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
3397			}
3398		}
3399
3400		foreach_node(MergeAction, action, parsetree->mergeActionList)
3401		{
3402			if (action->commandType == CMD_INSERT ||
3403				action->commandType == CMD_UPDATE)
3404			{
3405				foreach_node(TargetEntry, tle, action->targetList)
3406				{
3407					if (!tle->resjunk)
3408						modified_cols = bms_add_member(modified_cols,
3409													   tle->resno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
3410				}
3411			}
3412		}
3413
3414		auto_update_detail = view_cols_are_auto_updatable(viewquery,
3415														  modified_cols,
3416														  NULL,
3417														  &non_updatable_col);
3418		if (auto_update_detail)
3419		{
3420			/*
3421			 * This is a different error, caused by an attempt to update a
3422			 * non-updatable column in an otherwise updatable view.
3423			 */
3424			switch (parsetree->commandType)
3425			{
3426				case CMD_INSERT:
3427					ereport(ERROR,
3428							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3429							 errmsg("cannot insert into column \"%s\" of view \"%s\"",
3430									non_updatable_col,
3431									RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3432							 errdetail_internal("%s", _(auto_update_detail))));
3433					break;
3434				case CMD_UPDATE:
3435					ereport(ERROR,
3436							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3437							 errmsg("cannot update column \"%s\" of view \"%s\"",
3438									non_updatable_col,
3439									RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3440							 errdetail_internal("%s", _(auto_update_detail))));
3441					break;
3442				case CMD_MERGE:
3443					ereport(ERROR,
3444							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3445							 errmsg("cannot merge into column \"%s\" of view \"%s\"",
3446									non_updatable_col,
3447									RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3448							 errdetail_internal("%s", _(auto_update_detail))));
3449					break;
3450				default:
3451					elog(ERROR, "unrecognized CmdType: %d",
3452						 (int) parsetree->commandType);
3453					break;
3454			}
3455		}
3456	}
3457
3458	/*
3459	 * For MERGE, there must not be any INSTEAD OF triggers on an otherwise
3460	 * updatable view.  The caller already checked that there isn't a full set
3461	 * of INSTEAD OF triggers, so this is to guard against having a partial
3462	 * set (mixing auto-update and trigger-update actions in a single command
3463	 * isn't supported).
3464	 */
3465	if (parsetree->commandType == CMD_MERGE)
3466	{
3467		foreach_node(MergeAction, action, parsetree->mergeActionList)
3468		{
3469			if (action->commandType != CMD_NOTHING &&
3470				view_has_instead_trigger(view, action->commandType, NIL))
3471				ereport(ERROR,
3472						errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3473						errmsg("cannot merge into view \"%s\"",
3474							   RelationGetRelationName(view)),
3475						errdetail("MERGE is not supported for views with INSTEAD OF triggers for some actions but not all."),
3476						errhint("To enable merging into the view, either provide a full set of INSTEAD OF triggers or drop the existing INSTEAD OF triggers."));
3477		}
3478	}
3479
3480	/*
3481	 * If we get here, view_query_is_auto_updatable() has verified that the
3482	 * view contains a single base relation.
3483	 */
3484	Assert(list_length(viewquery->jointree->fromlist) == 1);
3485	rtr = linitial_node(RangeTblRef, viewquery->jointree->fromlist);
3486
3487	base_rt_index = rtr->rtindex;
3488	base_rte = rt_fetch(base_rt_index, viewquery->rtable);
3489	Assert(base_rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
3490	base_perminfo = getRTEPermissionInfo(viewquery->rteperminfos, base_rte);
3491
3492	/*
3493	 * Up to now, the base relation hasn't been touched at all in our query.
3494	 * We need to acquire lock on it before we try to do anything with it.
3495	 * (The subsequent recursive call of RewriteQuery will suppose that we
3496	 * already have the right lock!)  Since it will become the query target
3497	 * relation, RowExclusiveLock is always the right thing.
3498	 */
3499	base_rel = table_open(base_rte->relid, RowExclusiveLock);
3500
3501	/*
3502	 * While we have the relation open, update the RTE's relkind, just in case
3503	 * it changed since this view was made (cf. AcquireRewriteLocks).
3504	 */
3505	base_rte->relkind = base_rel->rd_rel->relkind;
3506
3507	/*
3508	 * If the view query contains any sublink subqueries then we need to also
3509	 * acquire locks on any relations they refer to.  We know that there won't
3510	 * be any subqueries in the range table or CTEs, so we can skip those, as
3511	 * in AcquireRewriteLocks.
3512	 */
3513	if (viewquery->hasSubLinks)
3514	{
3515		acquireLocksOnSubLinks_context context;
3516
3517		context.for_execute = true;
3518		query_tree_walker(viewquery, acquireLocksOnSubLinks, &context,
3519						  QTW_IGNORE_RC_SUBQUERIES);
3520	}
3521
3522	/*
3523	 * Create a new target RTE describing the base relation, and add it to the
3524	 * outer query's rangetable.  (What's happening in the next few steps is
3525	 * very much like what the planner would do to "pull up" the view into the
3526	 * outer query.  Perhaps someday we should refactor things enough so that
3527	 * we can share code with the planner.)
3528	 *
3529	 * Be sure to set rellockmode to the correct thing for the target table.
3530	 * Since we copied the whole viewquery above, we can just scribble on
3531	 * base_rte instead of copying it.
3532	 */
3533	new_rte = base_rte;
3534	new_rte->rellockmode = RowExclusiveLock;
3535
3536	parsetree->rtable = lappend(parsetree->rtable, new_rte);
3537	new_rt_index = list_length(parsetree->rtable);
3538
3539	/*
3540	 * INSERTs never inherit.  For UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE, we use the view
3541	 * query's inheritance flag for the base relation.
3542	 */
3543	if (parsetree->commandType == CMD_INSERT)
3544		new_rte->inh = false;
3545
3546	/*
3547	 * Adjust the view's targetlist Vars to reference the new target RTE, ie
3548	 * make their varnos be new_rt_index instead of base_rt_index.  There can
3549	 * be no Vars for other rels in the tlist, so this is sufficient to pull
3550	 * up the tlist expressions for use in the outer query.  The tlist will
3551	 * provide the replacement expressions used by ReplaceVarsFromTargetList
3552	 * below.
3553	 */
3554	view_targetlist = viewquery->targetList;
3555
3556	ChangeVarNodes((Node *) view_targetlist,
3557				   base_rt_index,
3558				   new_rt_index,
3559				   0);
3560
3561	/*
3562	 * If the view has "security_invoker" set, mark the new target relation
3563	 * for the permissions checks that we want to enforce against the query
3564	 * caller. Otherwise we want to enforce them against the view owner.
3565	 *
3566	 * At the relation level, require the same INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
3567	 * permissions that the query caller needs against the view.  We drop the
3568	 * ACL_SELECT bit that is presumably in new_perminfo->requiredPerms
3569	 * initially.
3570	 *
3571	 * Note: the original view's RTEPermissionInfo remains in the query's
3572	 * rteperminfos so that the executor still performs appropriate
3573	 * permissions checks for the query caller's use of the view.
3574	 *
3575	 * Disregard the perminfo in viewquery->rteperminfos that the base_rte
3576	 * would currently be pointing at, because we'd like it to point now to a
3577	 * new one that will be filled below.  Must set perminfoindex to 0 to not
3578	 * trip over the Assert in addRTEPermissionInfo().
3579	 */
3580	new_rte->perminfoindex = 0;
3581	new_perminfo = addRTEPermissionInfo(&parsetree->rteperminfos, new_rte);
3582	if (RelationHasSecurityInvoker(view))
3583		new_perminfo->checkAsUser = InvalidOid;
3584	else
3585		new_perminfo->checkAsUser = view->rd_rel->relowner;
3586	new_perminfo->requiredPerms = view_perminfo->requiredPerms;
3587
3588	/*
3589	 * Now for the per-column permissions bits.
3590	 *
3591	 * Initially, new_perminfo (base_perminfo) contains selectedCols
3592	 * permission check bits for all base-rel columns referenced by the view,
3593	 * but since the view is a SELECT query its insertedCols/updatedCols is
3594	 * empty.  We set insertedCols and updatedCols to include all the columns
3595	 * the outer query is trying to modify, adjusting the column numbers as
3596	 * needed.  But we leave selectedCols as-is, so the view owner must have
3597	 * read permission for all columns used in the view definition, even if
3598	 * some of them are not read by the outer query.  We could try to limit
3599	 * selectedCols to only columns used in the transformed query, but that
3600	 * does not correspond to what happens in ordinary SELECT usage of a view:
3601	 * all referenced columns must have read permission, even if optimization
3602	 * finds that some of them can be discarded during query transformation.
3603	 * The flattening we're doing here is an optional optimization, too.  (If
3604	 * you are unpersuaded and want to change this, note that applying
3605	 * adjust_view_column_set to view_perminfo->selectedCols is clearly *not*
3606	 * the right answer, since that neglects base-rel columns used in the
3607	 * view's WHERE quals.)
3608	 *
3609	 * This step needs the modified view targetlist, so we have to do things
3610	 * in this order.
3611	 */
3612	Assert(bms_is_empty(new_perminfo->insertedCols) &&
3613		   bms_is_empty(new_perminfo->updatedCols));
3614
3615	new_perminfo->selectedCols = base_perminfo->selectedCols;
3616
3617	new_perminfo->insertedCols =
3618		adjust_view_column_set(view_perminfo->insertedCols, view_targetlist);
3619
3620	new_perminfo->updatedCols =
3621		adjust_view_column_set(view_perminfo->updatedCols, view_targetlist);
3622
3623	/*
3624	 * Move any security barrier quals from the view RTE onto the new target
3625	 * RTE.  Any such quals should now apply to the new target RTE and will
3626	 * not reference the original view RTE in the rewritten query.
3627	 */
3628	new_rte->securityQuals = view_rte->securityQuals;
3629	view_rte->securityQuals = NIL;
3630
3631	/*
3632	 * Now update all Vars in the outer query that reference the view to
3633	 * reference the appropriate column of the base relation instead.
3634	 */
3635	parsetree = (Query *)
3636		ReplaceVarsFromTargetList((Node *) parsetree,
3637								  parsetree->resultRelation,
3638								  0,
3639								  view_rte,
3640								  view_targetlist,
3641								  new_rt_index,
3642								  REPLACEVARS_REPORT_ERROR,
3643								  0,
3644								  NULL);
3645
3646	/*
3647	 * Update all other RTI references in the query that point to the view
3648	 * (for example, parsetree->resultRelation itself) to point to the new
3649	 * base relation instead.  Vars will not be affected since none of them
3650	 * reference parsetree->resultRelation any longer.
3651	 */
3652	ChangeVarNodes((Node *) parsetree,
3653				   parsetree->resultRelation,
3654				   new_rt_index,
3655				   0);
3656	Assert(parsetree->resultRelation == new_rt_index);
3657
3658	/*
3659	 * For INSERT/UPDATE we must also update resnos in the targetlist to refer
3660	 * to columns of the base relation, since those indicate the target
3661	 * columns to be affected.  Similarly, for MERGE we must update the resnos
3662	 * in the merge action targetlists of any INSERT/UPDATE actions.
3663	 *
3664	 * Note that this destroys the resno ordering of the targetlists, but that
3665	 * will be fixed when we recurse through RewriteQuery, which will invoke
3666	 * rewriteTargetListIU again on the updated targetlists.
3667	 */
3668	if (parsetree->commandType != CMD_DELETE)
3669	{
3670		foreach(lc, parsetree->targetList)
3671		{
3672			TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
3673			TargetEntry *view_tle;
3674
3675			if (tle->resjunk)
3676				continue;
3677
3678			view_tle = get_tle_by_resno(view_targetlist, tle->resno);
3679			if (view_tle != NULL && !view_tle->resjunk && IsA(view_tle->expr, Var))
3680				tle->resno = ((Var *) view_tle->expr)->varattno;
3681			else
3682				elog(ERROR, "attribute number %d not found in view targetlist",
3683					 tle->resno);
3684		}
3685
3686		foreach_node(MergeAction, action, parsetree->mergeActionList)
3687		{
3688			if (action->commandType == CMD_INSERT ||
3689				action->commandType == CMD_UPDATE)
3690			{
3691				foreach_node(TargetEntry, tle, action->targetList)
3692				{
3693					TargetEntry *view_tle;
3694
3695					if (tle->resjunk)
3696						continue;
3697
3698					view_tle = get_tle_by_resno(view_targetlist, tle->resno);
3699					if (view_tle != NULL && !view_tle->resjunk && IsA(view_tle->expr, Var))
3700						tle->resno = ((Var *) view_tle->expr)->varattno;
3701					else
3702						elog(ERROR, "attribute number %d not found in view targetlist",
3703							 tle->resno);
3704				}
3705			}
3706		}
3707	}
3708
3709	/*
3710	 * For INSERT .. ON CONFLICT .. DO UPDATE, we must also update assorted
3711	 * stuff in the onConflict data structure.
3712	 */
3713	if (parsetree->onConflict &&
3714		parsetree->onConflict->action == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE)
3715	{
3716		Index		old_exclRelIndex,
3717					new_exclRelIndex;
3718		ParseNamespaceItem *new_exclNSItem;
3719		RangeTblEntry *new_exclRte;
3720		List	   *tmp_tlist;
3721
3722		/*
3723		 * Like the INSERT/UPDATE code above, update the resnos in the
3724		 * auxiliary UPDATE targetlist to refer to columns of the base
3725		 * relation.
3726		 */
3727		foreach(lc, parsetree->onConflict->onConflictSet)
3728		{
3729			TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
3730			TargetEntry *view_tle;
3731
3732			if (tle->resjunk)
3733				continue;
3734
3735			view_tle = get_tle_by_resno(view_targetlist, tle->resno);
3736			if (view_tle != NULL && !view_tle->resjunk && IsA(view_tle->expr, Var))
3737				tle->resno = ((Var *) view_tle->expr)->varattno;
3738			else
3739				elog(ERROR, "attribute number %d not found in view targetlist",
3740					 tle->resno);
3741		}
3742
3743		/*
3744		 * Also, create a new RTE for the EXCLUDED pseudo-relation, using the
3745		 * query's new base rel (which may well have a different column list
3746		 * from the view, hence we need a new column alias list).  This should
3747		 * match transformOnConflictClause.  In particular, note that the
3748		 * relkind is set to composite to signal that we're not dealing with
3749		 * an actual relation.
3750		 */
3751		old_exclRelIndex = parsetree->onConflict->exclRelIndex;
3752
3753		new_exclNSItem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(make_parsestate(NULL),
3754													   base_rel,
3755													   RowExclusiveLock,
3756													   makeAlias("excluded", NIL),
3757													   false, false);
3758		new_exclRte = new_exclNSItem->p_rte;
3759		new_exclRte->relkind = RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE;
3760		/* Ignore the RTEPermissionInfo that would've been added. */
3761		new_exclRte->perminfoindex = 0;
3762
3763		parsetree->rtable = lappend(parsetree->rtable, new_exclRte);
3764		new_exclRelIndex = parsetree->onConflict->exclRelIndex =
3765			list_length(parsetree->rtable);
3766
3767		/*
3768		 * Replace the targetlist for the EXCLUDED pseudo-relation with a new
3769		 * one, representing the columns from the new base relation.
3770		 */
3771		parsetree->onConflict->exclRelTlist =
3772			BuildOnConflictExcludedTargetlist(base_rel, new_exclRelIndex);
3773
3774		/*
3775		 * Update all Vars in the ON CONFLICT clause that refer to the old
3776		 * EXCLUDED pseudo-relation.  We want to use the column mappings
3777		 * defined in the view targetlist, but we need the outputs to refer to
3778		 * the new EXCLUDED pseudo-relation rather than the new target RTE.
3779		 * Also notice that "EXCLUDED.*" will be expanded using the view's
3780		 * rowtype, which seems correct.
3781		 */
3782		tmp_tlist = copyObject(view_targetlist);
3783
3784		ChangeVarNodes((Node *) tmp_tlist, new_rt_index,
3785					   new_exclRelIndex, 0);
3786
3787		parsetree->onConflict = (OnConflictExpr *)
3788			ReplaceVarsFromTargetList((Node *) parsetree->onConflict,
3789									  old_exclRelIndex,
3790									  0,
3791									  view_rte,
3792									  tmp_tlist,
3793									  new_rt_index,
3794									  REPLACEVARS_REPORT_ERROR,
3795									  0,
3796									  &parsetree->hasSubLinks);
3797	}
3798
3799	/*
3800	 * For UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE, pull up any WHERE quals from the view.  We
3801	 * know that any Vars in the quals must reference the one base relation,
3802	 * so we need only adjust their varnos to reference the new target (just
3803	 * the same as we did with the view targetlist).
3804	 *
3805	 * If it's a security-barrier view, its WHERE quals must be applied before
3806	 * quals from the outer query, so we attach them to the RTE as security
3807	 * barrier quals rather than adding them to the main WHERE clause.
3808	 *
3809	 * For INSERT, the view's quals can be ignored in the main query.
3810	 */
3811	if (parsetree->commandType != CMD_INSERT &&
3812		viewquery->jointree->quals != NULL)
3813	{
3814		Node	   *viewqual = (Node *) viewquery->jointree->quals;
3815
3816		/*
3817		 * Even though we copied viewquery already at the top of this
3818		 * function, we must duplicate the viewqual again here, because we may
3819		 * need to use the quals again below for a WithCheckOption clause.
3820		 */
3821		viewqual = copyObject(viewqual);
3822
3823		ChangeVarNodes(viewqual, base_rt_index, new_rt_index, 0);
3824
3825		if (RelationIsSecurityView(view))
3826		{
3827			/*
3828			 * The view's quals go in front of existing barrier quals: those
3829			 * would have come from an outer level of security-barrier view,
3830			 * and so must get evaluated later.
3831			 *
3832			 * Note: the parsetree has been mutated, so the new_rte pointer is
3833			 * stale and needs to be re-computed.
3834			 */
3835			new_rte = rt_fetch(new_rt_index, parsetree->rtable);
3836			new_rte->securityQuals = lcons(viewqual, new_rte->securityQuals);
3837
3838			/*
3839			 * Do not set parsetree->hasRowSecurity, because these aren't RLS
3840			 * conditions (they aren't affected by enabling/disabling RLS).
3841			 */
3842
3843			/*
3844			 * Make sure that the query is marked correctly if the added qual
3845			 * has sublinks.
3846			 */
3847			if (!parsetree->hasSubLinks)
3848				parsetree->hasSubLinks = checkExprHasSubLink(viewqual);
3849		}
3850		else
3851			AddQual(parsetree, (Node *) viewqual);
3852	}
3853
3854	/*
3855	 * For INSERT/UPDATE (or MERGE containing INSERT/UPDATE), if the view has
3856	 * the WITH CHECK OPTION, or any parent view specified WITH CASCADED CHECK
3857	 * OPTION, add the quals from the view to the query's withCheckOptions
3858	 * list.
3859	 */
3860	if (insert_or_update)
3861	{
3862		bool		has_wco = RelationHasCheckOption(view);
3863		bool		cascaded = RelationHasCascadedCheckOption(view);
3864
3865		/*
3866		 * If the parent view has a cascaded check option, treat this view as
3867		 * if it also had a cascaded check option.
3868		 *
3869		 * New WithCheckOptions are added to the start of the list, so if
3870		 * there is a cascaded check option, it will be the first item in the
3871		 * list.
3872		 */
3873		if (parsetree->withCheckOptions != NIL)
3874		{
3875			WithCheckOption *parent_wco =
3876				(WithCheckOption *) linitial(parsetree->withCheckOptions);
3877
3878			if (parent_wco->cascaded)
3879			{
3880				has_wco = true;
3881				cascaded = true;
3882			}
3883		}
3884
3885		/*
3886		 * Add the new WithCheckOption to the start of the list, so that
3887		 * checks on inner views are run before checks on outer views, as
3888		 * required by the SQL standard.
3889		 *
3890		 * If the new check is CASCADED, we need to add it even if this view
3891		 * has no quals, since there may be quals on child views.  A LOCAL
3892		 * check can be omitted if this view has no quals.
3893		 */
3894		if (has_wco && (cascaded || viewquery->jointree->quals != NULL))
3895		{
3896			WithCheckOption *wco;
3897
3898			wco = makeNode(WithCheckOption);
3899			wco->kind = WCO_VIEW_CHECK;
3900			wco->relname = pstrdup(RelationGetRelationName(view));
3901			wco->polname = NULL;
3902			wco->qual = NULL;
3903			wco->cascaded = cascaded;
3904
3905			parsetree->withCheckOptions = lcons(wco,
3906												parsetree->withCheckOptions);
3907
3908			if (viewquery->jointree->quals != NULL)
3909			{
3910				wco->qual = (Node *) viewquery->jointree->quals;
3911				ChangeVarNodes(wco->qual, base_rt_index, new_rt_index, 0);
3912
3913				/*
3914				 * For INSERT, make sure that the query is marked correctly if
3915				 * the added qual has sublinks.  This can be skipped for
3916				 * UPDATE/MERGE, since the same qual will have already been
3917				 * added above, and the check will already have been done.
3918				 */
3919				if (!parsetree->hasSubLinks &&
3920					parsetree->commandType == CMD_INSERT)
3921					parsetree->hasSubLinks = checkExprHasSubLink(wco->qual);
3922			}
3923		}
3924	}
3925
3926	table_close(base_rel, NoLock);
3927
3928	return parsetree;
3929}
3930
3931
3932/*
3933 * RewriteQuery -
3934 *	  rewrites the query and apply the rules again on the queries rewritten
3935 *
3936 * rewrite_events is a list of open query-rewrite actions, so we can detect
3937 * infinite recursion.
3938 *
3939 * orig_rt_length is the length of the originating query's rtable, for product
3940 * queries created by fireRules(), and 0 otherwise.  This is used to skip any
3941 * already-processed VALUES RTEs from the original query.
3942 *
3943 * num_ctes_processed is the number of CTEs at the end of the query's cteList
3944 * that have already been rewritten, and must not be rewritten again.
3945 */
3946static List *
3947RewriteQuery(Query *parsetree, List *rewrite_events, int orig_rt_length,
3948			 int num_ctes_processed)
3949{
3950	CmdType		event = parsetree->commandType;
3951	bool		instead = false;
3952	bool		returning = false;
3953	bool		updatableview = false;
3954	Query	   *qual_product = NULL;
3955	List	   *rewritten = NIL;
3956	ListCell   *lc1;
3957
3958	/*
3959	 * First, recursively process any insert/update/delete/merge statements in
3960	 * WITH clauses.  (We have to do this first because the WITH clauses may
3961	 * get copied into rule actions below.)
3962	 *
3963	 * Any new WITH clauses from rule actions are processed when we recurse
3964	 * into product queries below.  However, when recursing, we must take care
3965	 * to avoid rewriting a CTE query more than once (because expanding
3966	 * generated columns in the targetlist more than once would fail).  Since
3967	 * new CTEs from product queries are added to the start of the list (see
3968	 * rewriteRuleAction), we just skip the last num_ctes_processed items.
3969	 */
3970	foreach(lc1, parsetree->cteList)
3971	{
3972		CommonTableExpr *cte = lfirst_node(CommonTableExpr, lc1);
3973		Query	   *ctequery = castNode(Query, cte->ctequery);
3974		int			i = foreach_current_index(lc1);
3975		List	   *newstuff;
3976
3977		/* Skip already-processed CTEs at the end of the list */
3978		if (i >= list_length(parsetree->cteList) - num_ctes_processed)
3979			break;
3980
3981		if (ctequery->commandType == CMD_SELECT)
3982			continue;
3983
3984		newstuff = RewriteQuery(ctequery, rewrite_events, 0, 0);
3985
3986		/*
3987		 * Currently we can only handle unconditional, single-statement DO
3988		 * INSTEAD rules correctly; we have to get exactly one non-utility
3989		 * Query out of the rewrite operation to stuff back into the CTE node.
3990		 */
3991		if (list_length(newstuff) == 1)
3992		{
3993			/* Must check it's not a utility command */
3994			ctequery = linitial_node(Query, newstuff);
3995			if (!(ctequery->commandType == CMD_SELECT ||
3996				  ctequery->commandType == CMD_UPDATE ||
3997				  ctequery->commandType == CMD_INSERT ||
3998				  ctequery->commandType == CMD_DELETE ||
3999				  ctequery->commandType == CMD_MERGE))
4000			{
4001				/*
4002				 * Currently it could only be NOTIFY; this error message will
4003				 * need work if we ever allow other utility commands in rules.
4004				 */
4005				ereport(ERROR,
4006						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4007						 errmsg("DO INSTEAD NOTIFY rules are not supported for data-modifying statements in WITH")));
4008			}
4009			/* WITH queries should never be canSetTag */
4010			Assert(!ctequery->canSetTag);
4011			/* Push the single Query back into the CTE node */
4012			cte->ctequery = (Node *) ctequery;
4013		}
4014		else if (newstuff == NIL)
4015		{
4016			ereport(ERROR,
4017					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4018					 errmsg("DO INSTEAD NOTHING rules are not supported for data-modifying statements in WITH")));
4019		}
4020		else
4021		{
4022			ListCell   *lc2;
4023
4024			/* examine queries to determine which error message to issue */
4025			foreach(lc2, newstuff)
4026			{
4027				Query	   *q = (Query *) lfirst(lc2);
4028
4029				if (q->querySource == QSRC_QUAL_INSTEAD_RULE)
4030					ereport(ERROR,
4031							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4032							 errmsg("conditional DO INSTEAD rules are not supported for data-modifying statements in WITH")));
4033				if (q->querySource == QSRC_NON_INSTEAD_RULE)
4034					ereport(ERROR,
4035							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4036							 errmsg("DO ALSO rules are not supported for data-modifying statements in WITH")));
4037			}
4038
4039			ereport(ERROR,
4040					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4041					 errmsg("multi-statement DO INSTEAD rules are not supported for data-modifying statements in WITH")));
4042		}
4043	}
4044	num_ctes_processed = list_length(parsetree->cteList);
4045
4046	/*
4047	 * If the statement is an insert, update, delete, or merge, adjust its
4048	 * targetlist as needed, and then fire INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE rules on it.
4049	 *
4050	 * SELECT rules are handled later when we have all the queries that should
4051	 * get executed.  Also, utilities aren't rewritten at all (do we still
4052	 * need that check?)
4053	 */
4054	if (event != CMD_SELECT && event != CMD_UTILITY)
4055	{
4056		int			result_relation;
4057		RangeTblEntry *rt_entry;
4058		Relation	rt_entry_relation;
4059		List	   *locks;
4060		int			product_orig_rt_length;
4061		List	   *product_queries;
4062		bool		hasUpdate = false;
4063		int			values_rte_index = 0;
4064		bool		defaults_remaining = false;
4065
4066		result_relation = parsetree->resultRelation;
4067		Assert(result_relation != 0);
4068		rt_entry = rt_fetch(result_relation, parsetree->rtable);
4069		Assert(rt_entry->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
4070
4071		/*
4072		 * We can use NoLock here since either the parser or
4073		 * AcquireRewriteLocks should have locked the rel already.
4074		 */
4075		rt_entry_relation = table_open(rt_entry->relid, NoLock);
4076
4077		/*
4078		 * Rewrite the targetlist as needed for the command type.
4079		 */
4080		if (event == CMD_INSERT)
4081		{
4082			ListCell   *lc2;
4083			RangeTblEntry *values_rte = NULL;
4084
4085			/*
4086			 * Test if it's a multi-row INSERT ... VALUES (...), (...), ... by
4087			 * looking for a VALUES RTE in the fromlist.  For product queries,
4088			 * we must ignore any already-processed VALUES RTEs from the
4089			 * original query.  These appear at the start of the rangetable.
4090			 */
4091			foreach(lc2, parsetree->jointree->fromlist)
4092			{
4093				RangeTblRef *rtr = (RangeTblRef *) lfirst(lc2);
4094
4095				if (IsA(rtr, RangeTblRef) && rtr->rtindex > orig_rt_length)
4096				{
4097					RangeTblEntry *rte = rt_fetch(rtr->rtindex,
4098												  parsetree->rtable);
4099
4100					if (rte->rtekind == RTE_VALUES)
4101					{
4102						/* should not find more than one VALUES RTE */
4103						if (values_rte != NULL)
4104							elog(ERROR, "more than one VALUES RTE found");
4105
4106						values_rte = rte;
4107						values_rte_index = rtr->rtindex;
4108					}
4109				}
4110			}
4111
4112			if (values_rte)
4113			{
4114				Bitmapset  *unused_values_attrnos = NULL;
4115
4116				/* Process the main targetlist ... */
4117				parsetree->targetList = rewriteTargetListIU(parsetree->targetList,
4118															parsetree->commandType,
4119															parsetree->override,
4120															rt_entry_relation,
4121															values_rte,
4122															values_rte_index,
4123															&unused_values_attrnos);
4124				/* ... and the VALUES expression lists */
4125				if (!rewriteValuesRTE(parsetree, values_rte, values_rte_index,
4126									  rt_entry_relation,
4127									  unused_values_attrnos))
4128					defaults_remaining = true;
4129			}
4130			else
4131			{
4132				/* Process just the main targetlist */
4133				parsetree->targetList =
4134					rewriteTargetListIU(parsetree->targetList,
4135										parsetree->commandType,
4136										parsetree->override,
4137										rt_entry_relation,
4138										NULL, 0, NULL);
4139			}
4140
4141			if (parsetree->onConflict &&
4142				parsetree->onConflict->action == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE)
4143			{
4144				parsetree->onConflict->onConflictSet =
4145					rewriteTargetListIU(parsetree->onConflict->onConflictSet,
4146										CMD_UPDATE,
4147										parsetree->override,
4148										rt_entry_relation,
4149										NULL, 0, NULL);
4150			}
4151		}
4152		else if (event == CMD_UPDATE)
4153		{
4154			Assert(parsetree->override == OVERRIDING_NOT_SET);
4155			parsetree->targetList =
4156				rewriteTargetListIU(parsetree->targetList,
4157									parsetree->commandType,
4158									parsetree->override,
4159									rt_entry_relation,
4160									NULL, 0, NULL);
4161		}
4162		else if (event == CMD_MERGE)
4163		{
4164			Assert(parsetree->override == OVERRIDING_NOT_SET);
4165
4166			/*
4167			 * Rewrite each action targetlist separately
4168			 */
4169			foreach(lc1, parsetree->mergeActionList)
4170			{
4171				MergeAction *action = (MergeAction *) lfirst(lc1);
4172
4173				switch (action->commandType)
4174				{
4175					case CMD_NOTHING:
4176					case CMD_DELETE:	/* Nothing to do here */
4177						break;
4178					case CMD_UPDATE:
4179					case CMD_INSERT:
4180
4181						/*
4182						 * MERGE actions do not permit multi-row INSERTs, so
4183						 * there is no VALUES RTE to deal with here.
4184						 */
4185						action->targetList =
4186							rewriteTargetListIU(action->targetList,
4187												action->commandType,
4188												action->override,
4189												rt_entry_relation,
4190												NULL, 0, NULL);
4191						break;
4192					default:
4193						elog(ERROR, "unrecognized commandType: %d", action->commandType);
4194						break;
4195				}
4196			}
4197		}
4198		else if (event == CMD_DELETE)
4199		{
4200			/* Nothing to do here */
4201		}
4202		else
4203			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized commandType: %d", (int) event);
4204
4205		/*
4206		 * Collect and apply the appropriate rules.
4207		 */
4208		locks = matchLocks(event, rt_entry_relation,
4209						   result_relation, parsetree, &hasUpdate);
4210
4211		product_orig_rt_length = list_length(parsetree->rtable);
4212		product_queries = fireRules(parsetree,
4213									result_relation,
4214									event,
4215									locks,
4216									&instead,
4217									&returning,
4218									&qual_product);
4219
4220		/*
4221		 * If we have a VALUES RTE with any remaining untouched DEFAULT items,
4222		 * and we got any product queries, finalize the VALUES RTE for each
4223		 * product query (replacing the remaining DEFAULT items with NULLs).
4224		 * We don't do this for the original query, because we know that it
4225		 * must be an auto-insert on a view, and so should use the base
4226		 * relation's defaults for any remaining DEFAULT items.
4227		 */
4228		if (defaults_remaining && product_queries != NIL)
4229		{
4230			ListCell   *n;
4231
4232			/*
4233			 * Each product query has its own copy of the VALUES RTE at the
4234			 * same index in the rangetable, so we must finalize each one.
4235			 *
4236			 * Note that if the product query is an INSERT ... SELECT, then
4237			 * the VALUES RTE will be at the same index in the SELECT part of
4238			 * the product query rather than the top-level product query
4239			 * itself.
4240			 */
4241			foreach(n, product_queries)
4242			{
4243				Query	   *pt = (Query *) lfirst(n);
4244				RangeTblEntry *values_rte;
4245
4246				if (pt->commandType == CMD_INSERT &&
4247					pt->jointree && IsA(pt->jointree, FromExpr) &&
4248					list_length(pt->jointree->fromlist) == 1)
4249				{
4250					Node	   *jtnode = (Node *) linitial(pt->jointree->fromlist);
4251
4252					if (IsA(jtnode, RangeTblRef))
4253					{
4254						int			rtindex = ((RangeTblRef *) jtnode)->rtindex;
4255						RangeTblEntry *src_rte = rt_fetch(rtindex, pt->rtable);
4256
4257						if (src_rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY &&
4258							src_rte->subquery &&
4259							IsA(src_rte->subquery, Query) &&
4260							src_rte->subquery->commandType == CMD_SELECT)
4261							pt = src_rte->subquery;
4262					}
4263				}
4264
4265				values_rte = rt_fetch(values_rte_index, pt->rtable);
4266				if (values_rte->rtekind != RTE_VALUES)
4267					elog(ERROR, "failed to find VALUES RTE in product query");
4268
4269				rewriteValuesRTEToNulls(pt, values_rte);
4270			}
4271		}
4272
4273		/*
4274		 * If there was no unqualified INSTEAD rule, and the target relation
4275		 * is a view without any INSTEAD OF triggers, see if the view can be
4276		 * automatically updated.  If so, we perform the necessary query
4277		 * transformation here and add the resulting query to the
4278		 * product_queries list, so that it gets recursively rewritten if
4279		 * necessary.  For MERGE, the view must be automatically updatable if
4280		 * any of the merge actions lack a corresponding INSTEAD OF trigger.
4281		 *
4282		 * If the view cannot be automatically updated, we throw an error here
4283		 * which is OK since the query would fail at runtime anyway.  Throwing
4284		 * the error here is preferable to the executor check since we have
4285		 * more detailed information available about why the view isn't
4286		 * updatable.
4287		 */
4288		if (!instead &&
4289			rt_entry_relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW &&
4290			!view_has_instead_trigger(rt_entry_relation, event,
4291									  parsetree->mergeActionList))
4292		{
4293			/*
4294			 * If there were any qualified INSTEAD rules, don't allow the view
4295			 * to be automatically updated (an unqualified INSTEAD rule or
4296			 * INSTEAD OF trigger is required).
4297			 */
4298			if (qual_product != NULL)
4299				error_view_not_updatable(rt_entry_relation,
4300										 parsetree->commandType,
4301										 parsetree->mergeActionList,
4302										 gettext_noop("Views with conditional DO INSTEAD rules are not automatically updatable."));
4303
4304			/*
4305			 * Attempt to rewrite the query to automatically update the view.
4306			 * This throws an error if the view can't be automatically
4307			 * updated.
4308			 */
4309			parsetree = rewriteTargetView(parsetree, rt_entry_relation);
4310
4311			/*
4312			 * At this point product_queries contains any DO ALSO rule
4313			 * actions. Add the rewritten query before or after those.  This
4314			 * must match the handling the original query would have gotten
4315			 * below, if we allowed it to be included again.
4316			 */
4317			if (parsetree->commandType == CMD_INSERT)
4318				product_queries = lcons(parsetree, product_queries);
4319			else
4320				product_queries = lappend(product_queries, parsetree);
4321
4322			/*
4323			 * Set the "instead" flag, as if there had been an unqualified
4324			 * INSTEAD, to prevent the original query from being included a
4325			 * second time below.  The transformation will have rewritten any
4326			 * RETURNING list, so we can also set "returning" to forestall
4327			 * throwing an error below.
4328			 */
4329			instead = true;
4330			returning = true;
4331			updatableview = true;
4332		}
4333
4334		/*
4335		 * If we got any product queries, recursively rewrite them --- but
4336		 * first check for recursion!
4337		 */
4338		if (product_queries != NIL)
4339		{
4340			ListCell   *n;
4341			rewrite_event *rev;
4342
4343			foreach(n, rewrite_events)
4344			{
4345				rev = (rewrite_event *) lfirst(n);
4346				if (rev->relation == RelationGetRelid(rt_entry_relation) &&
4347					rev->event == event)
4348					ereport(ERROR,
4349							(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
4350							 errmsg("infinite recursion detected in rules for relation \"%s\"",
4351									RelationGetRelationName(rt_entry_relation))));
4352			}
4353
4354			rev = (rewrite_event *) palloc(sizeof(rewrite_event));
4355			rev->relation = RelationGetRelid(rt_entry_relation);
4356			rev->event = event;
4357			rewrite_events = lappend(rewrite_events, rev);
4358
4359			foreach(n, product_queries)
4360			{
4361				Query	   *pt = (Query *) lfirst(n);
4362				List	   *newstuff;
4363
4364				/*
4365				 * For an updatable view, pt might be the rewritten version of
4366				 * the original query, in which case we pass on orig_rt_length
4367				 * to finish processing any VALUES RTE it contained.
4368				 *
4369				 * Otherwise, we have a product query created by fireRules().
4370				 * Any VALUES RTEs from the original query have been fully
4371				 * processed, and must be skipped when we recurse.
4372				 */
4373				newstuff = RewriteQuery(pt, rewrite_events,
4374										pt == parsetree ?
4375										orig_rt_length :
4376										product_orig_rt_length,
4377										num_ctes_processed);
4378				rewritten = list_concat(rewritten, newstuff);
4379			}
4380
4381			rewrite_events = list_delete_last(rewrite_events);
4382		}
4383
4384		/*
4385		 * If there is an INSTEAD, and the original query has a RETURNING, we
4386		 * have to have found a RETURNING in the rule(s), else fail. (Because
4387		 * DefineQueryRewrite only allows RETURNING in unconditional INSTEAD
4388		 * rules, there's no need to worry whether the substituted RETURNING
4389		 * will actually be executed --- it must be.)
4390		 */
4391		if ((instead || qual_product != NULL) &&
4392			parsetree->returningList &&
4393			!returning)
4394		{
4395			switch (event)
4396			{
4397				case CMD_INSERT:
4398					ereport(ERROR,
4399							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4400							 errmsg("cannot perform INSERT RETURNING on relation \"%s\"",
4401									RelationGetRelationName(rt_entry_relation)),
4402							 errhint("You need an unconditional ON INSERT DO INSTEAD rule with a RETURNING clause.")));
4403					break;
4404				case CMD_UPDATE:
4405					ereport(ERROR,
4406							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4407							 errmsg("cannot perform UPDATE RETURNING on relation \"%s\"",
4408									RelationGetRelationName(rt_entry_relation)),
4409							 errhint("You need an unconditional ON UPDATE DO INSTEAD rule with a RETURNING clause.")));
4410					break;
4411				case CMD_DELETE:
4412					ereport(ERROR,
4413							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4414							 errmsg("cannot perform DELETE RETURNING on relation \"%s\"",
4415									RelationGetRelationName(rt_entry_relation)),
4416							 errhint("You need an unconditional ON DELETE DO INSTEAD rule with a RETURNING clause.")));
4417					break;
4418				default:
4419					elog(ERROR, "unrecognized commandType: %d",
4420						 (int) event);
4421					break;
4422			}
4423		}
4424
4425		/*
4426		 * Updatable views are supported by ON CONFLICT, so don't prevent that
4427		 * case from proceeding
4428		 */
