4429		if (parsetree->onConflict &&
4430			(product_queries != NIL || hasUpdate) &&
4431			!updatableview)
4432			ereport(ERROR,
4433					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4434					 errmsg("INSERT with ON CONFLICT clause cannot be used with table that has INSERT or UPDATE rules")));
4435
4436		table_close(rt_entry_relation, NoLock);
4437	}
4438
4439	/*
4440	 * For INSERTs, the original query is done first; for UPDATE/DELETE, it is
4441	 * done last.  This is needed because update and delete rule actions might
4442	 * not do anything if they are invoked after the update or delete is
4443	 * performed. The command counter increment between the query executions
4444	 * makes the deleted (and maybe the updated) tuples disappear so the scans
4445	 * for them in the rule actions cannot find them.
4446	 *
4447	 * If we found any unqualified INSTEAD, the original query is not done at
4448	 * all, in any form.  Otherwise, we add the modified form if qualified
4449	 * INSTEADs were found, else the unmodified form.
4450	 */
4451	if (!instead)
4452	{
4453		if (parsetree->commandType == CMD_INSERT)
4454		{
4455			if (qual_product != NULL)
4456				rewritten = lcons(qual_product, rewritten);
4457			else
4458				rewritten = lcons(parsetree, rewritten);
4459		}
4460		else
4461		{
4462			if (qual_product != NULL)
4463				rewritten = lappend(rewritten, qual_product);
4464			else
4465				rewritten = lappend(rewritten, parsetree);
4466		}
4467	}
4468
4469	/*
4470	 * If the original query has a CTE list, and we generated more than one
4471	 * non-utility result query, we have to fail because we'll have copied the
4472	 * CTE list into each result query.  That would break the expectation of
4473	 * single evaluation of CTEs.  This could possibly be fixed by
4474	 * restructuring so that a CTE list can be shared across multiple Query
4475	 * and PlannableStatement nodes.
4476	 */
4477	if (parsetree->cteList != NIL)
4478	{
4479		int			qcount = 0;
4480
4481		foreach(lc1, rewritten)
4482		{
4483			Query	   *q = (Query *) lfirst(lc1);
4484
4485			if (q->commandType != CMD_UTILITY)
4486				qcount++;
4487		}
4488		if (qcount > 1)
4489			ereport(ERROR,
4490					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4491					 errmsg("WITH cannot be used in a query that is rewritten by rules into multiple queries")));
4492	}
4493
4494	return rewritten;
4495}
4496
4497
4498/*
4499 * Get a table's generated columns
4500 *
4501 * If include_stored is true, both stored and virtual generated columns are
4502 * returned.  Otherwise, only virtual generated columns are returned.
4503 *
4504 * Returns a list of TargetEntry, one for each generated column, containing
4505 * the attribute numbers and generation expressions.
4506 */
4507static List *
4508get_generated_columns(Relation rel, int rt_index, bool include_stored)
4509{
4510	List	   *gen_cols = NIL;
4511	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
4512
4513	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
4514	if (tupdesc->constr &&
4515		(tupdesc->constr->has_generated_virtual ||
4516		 (include_stored && tupdesc->constr->has_generated_stored)))
4517	{
4518		for (int i = 0; i < tupdesc->natts; i++)
4519		{
4520			Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i);
4521
4522			if (attr->attgenerated == ATTRIBUTE_GENERATED_VIRTUAL ||
4523				(include_stored && attr->attgenerated == ATTRIBUTE_GENERATED_STORED))
4524			{
4525				Node	   *defexpr;
4526				TargetEntry *te;
4527
4528				defexpr = build_generation_expression(rel, i + 1);
4529				ChangeVarNodes(defexpr, 1, rt_index, 0);
4530
4531				te = makeTargetEntry((Expr *) defexpr, i + 1, 0, false);
4532				gen_cols = lappend(gen_cols, te);
4533			}
4534		}
4535	}
4536
4537	return gen_cols;
4538}
4539
4540/*
4541 * Expand virtual generated columns in an expression
4542 *
4543 * This is for expressions that are not part of a query, such as default
4544 * expressions or index predicates.  The rt_index is usually 1.
4545 */
4546Node *
4547expand_generated_columns_in_expr(Node *node, Relation rel, int rt_index)
4548{
4549	TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
4550
4551	if (tupdesc->constr && tupdesc->constr->has_generated_virtual)
4552	{
4553		RangeTblEntry *rte;
4554		List	   *vcols;
4555
4556		rte = makeNode(RangeTblEntry);
4557		/* eref needs to be set, but the actual name doesn't matter */
4558		rte->eref = makeAlias(RelationGetRelationName(rel), NIL);
4559		rte->rtekind = RTE_RELATION;
4560		rte->relid = RelationGetRelid(rel);
4561
4562		vcols = get_generated_columns(rel, rt_index, false);
4563
4564		if (vcols)
4565		{
4566			/*
4567			 * Passing NULL for outer_hasSubLinks is safe because generation
4568			 * expressions cannot contain SubLinks, so the replacement cannot
4569			 * introduce any.
4570			 */
4571			node = ReplaceVarsFromTargetList(node, rt_index, 0, rte, vcols, 0,
4572											 REPLACEVARS_CHANGE_VARNO, rt_index,
4573											 NULL);
4574		}
4575	}
4576
4577	return node;
4578}
4579
4580/*
4581 * Build the generation expression for a generated column.
4582 *
4583 * Error out if there is no generation expression found for the given column.
4584 */
4585Node *
4586build_generation_expression(Relation rel, int attrno)
4587{
4588	TupleDesc	rd_att = RelationGetDescr(rel);
4589	Form_pg_attribute att_tup = TupleDescAttr(rd_att, attrno - 1);
4590	Node	   *defexpr;
4591	Oid			attcollid;
4592
4593	Assert(rd_att->constr &&
4594		   (rd_att->constr->has_generated_virtual ||
4595			rd_att->constr->has_generated_stored));
4596	Assert(att_tup->attgenerated == ATTRIBUTE_GENERATED_VIRTUAL ||
4597		   att_tup->attgenerated == ATTRIBUTE_GENERATED_STORED);
4598
4599	defexpr = build_column_default(rel, attrno);
4600	if (defexpr == NULL)
4601		elog(ERROR, "no generation expression found for column number %d of table \"%s\"",
4602			 attrno, RelationGetRelationName(rel));
4603
4604	/*
4605	 * If the column definition has a collation and it is different from the
4606	 * collation of the generation expression, put a COLLATE clause around the
4607	 * expression.
4608	 */
4609	attcollid = att_tup->attcollation;
4610	if (attcollid && attcollid != exprCollation(defexpr))
4611	{
4612		CollateExpr *ce = makeNode(CollateExpr);
4613
4614		ce->arg = (Expr *) defexpr;
4615		ce->collOid = attcollid;
4616		ce->location = -1;
4617
4618		defexpr = (Node *) ce;
4619	}
4620
4621	return defexpr;
4622}
4623
4624
4625/*
4626 * QueryRewrite -
4627 *	  Primary entry point to the query rewriter.
4628 *	  Rewrite one query via query rewrite system, possibly returning 0
4629 *	  or many queries.
4630 *
4631 * NOTE: the parsetree must either have come straight from the parser,
4632 * or have been scanned by AcquireRewriteLocks to acquire suitable locks.
4633 */
4634List *
4635QueryRewrite(Query *parsetree)
4636{
4637	int64		input_query_id = parsetree->queryId;
4638	List	   *querylist;
4639	List	   *results;
4640	ListCell   *l;
4641	CmdType		origCmdType;
4642	bool		foundOriginalQuery;
4643	Query	   *lastInstead;
4644
4645	/*
4646	 * This function is only applied to top-level original queries
4647	 */
4648	Assert(parsetree->querySource == QSRC_ORIGINAL);
4649	Assert(parsetree->canSetTag);
4650
4651	/*
4652	 * Step 1
4653	 *
4654	 * Apply all non-SELECT rules possibly getting 0 or many queries
4655	 */
4656	querylist = RewriteQuery(parsetree, NIL, 0, 0);
4657
4658	/*
4659	 * Step 2
4660	 *
4661	 * Apply all the RIR rules on each query
4662	 *
4663	 * This is also a handy place to mark each query with the original queryId
4664	 */
4665	results = NIL;
4666	foreach(l, querylist)
4667	{
4668		Query	   *query = (Query *) lfirst(l);
4669
4670		query = fireRIRrules(query, NIL);
4671
4672		query->queryId = input_query_id;
4673
4674		results = lappend(results, query);
4675	}
4676
4677	/*
4678	 * Step 3
4679	 *
4680	 * Determine which, if any, of the resulting queries is supposed to set
4681	 * the command-result tag; and update the canSetTag fields accordingly.
4682	 *
4683	 * If the original query is still in the list, it sets the command tag.
4684	 * Otherwise, the last INSTEAD query of the same kind as the original is
4685	 * allowed to set the tag.  (Note these rules can leave us with no query
4686	 * setting the tag.  The tcop code has to cope with this by setting up a
4687	 * default tag based on the original un-rewritten query.)
4688	 *
4689	 * The Asserts verify that at most one query in the result list is marked
4690	 * canSetTag.  If we aren't checking asserts, we can fall out of the loop
4691	 * as soon as we find the original query.
4692	 */
4693	origCmdType = parsetree->commandType;
4694	foundOriginalQuery = false;
4695	lastInstead = NULL;
4696
4697	foreach(l, results)
4698	{
4699		Query	   *query = (Query *) lfirst(l);
4700
4701		if (query->querySource == QSRC_ORIGINAL)
4702		{
4703			Assert(query->canSetTag);
4704			Assert(!foundOriginalQuery);
4705			foundOriginalQuery = true;
4706#ifndef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
4707			break;
4708#endif
4709		}
4710		else
4711		{
4712			Assert(!query->canSetTag);
4713			if (query->commandType == origCmdType &&
4714				(query->querySource == QSRC_INSTEAD_RULE ||
4715				 query->querySource == QSRC_QUAL_INSTEAD_RULE))
4716				lastInstead = query;
4717		}
4718	}
4719
4720	if (!foundOriginalQuery && lastInstead != NULL)
4721		lastInstead->canSetTag = true;
4722
4723	return results;
4724}
